scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a verified disruption recovery through explicit lifecycle states and produced a movement or turnaround summary tied to aircraft, gate, baggage, ULD, passenger, crew, fueling, cleaning, and connection evidence.
Missing diversion, gate, notification, cargo, and handoff records correctly blocked progression and generated a prerequisite checklist.
The gate and equipment exception was reworked through owner and dependency routing without promising dispatch or on-time departure authority.
The lane refused closure without passenger, baggage, cargo, gate reset, and destination-owner evidence and preserved a reversible lifecycle state.
Missing baggage transfer scan and cargo manifest mismatch were treated as custody breaks and routed to adjacent owners without inventing proof.
Winter, NOTAM, tarmac-delay, gate, customs, security, and passenger-impact dependencies were escalated while safety, dispatch, and customer-service-plan boundaries remained intact.
Requests for departure release, customs cargo clearance, and contaminated-stand approval were refused and routed to dispatch, customs, and safety owners.
Irregular-operations terminology was normalized into artifacts, lifecycle state, dependencies, authority boundary, and next action.
Compensation approval and maintenance return-to-service signoff were rejected and routed to customer operations and maintenance owners.
Missing playbook, customer-service plan, tarmac-delay plan, owner map, SLA, airport rules, and retrieval hooks triggered low-confidence exception handling.
Conflicting public, carrier, airport, and station policy inputs were escalated for human resolution instead of confidently synthesized.
Tenant adaptation was limited to terminology and routing overlays and did not bypass evidence, handoff, safety, customs, or documentation checks.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to aviation and airport irregular-operations execution, disruption recovery, movement, custody, service-level, documentation, escalation, and closure evidence.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and all transportation-and-logistics abilities required by the issue contract.
